title: Clean Small Valve Ports With Compressed Air For A Clogged Air Line
summary: Use compressed air to clean debris from the small ports inside the valve when pneumatic troubleshooting identifies a clogged air line caused by contamination in the valve ports.
when_to_use: Use when troubleshooting a clogged air line and the source identifies debris in the small ports inside the valve as the likely cause.
not_for: Do not use as a complete pneumatic isolation or disassembly procedure; the source does not provide those details.
Do not use as a filtration system correction procedure; the source only notes that better filtration may be needed if contamination is common.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Inspect the valve ports, clean the ports with compressed air, and verify whether the clogged air line condition is cleared.

safety_notes: This procedure is not confirmed support-safe by the source.
The source does not provide pneumatic isolation, pressure release, disassembly, or lockout details.
Review site maintenance controls before performing compressed-air cleaning on the valve.

system_preconditions: A clogged air line condition has been identified during pneumatic troubleshooting.
Debris in the small ports inside the valve is being treated as the suspected cause.

visual_references.description: Reference diagram that may help identify tipper-related components while locating the affected valve area; not a valve-port cleaning figure.

steps.title: Identify the valve associated with the clogged air line
steps.purpose: Locate the valve referenced by the troubleshooting condition before inspection and cleaning.
steps.instruction: Identify the valve associated with the clogged air line so the small ports inside that valve can be inspected and cleaned.
steps.expected_result: The affected valve has been identified for inspection.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct valve associated with the clogged air line is located and accessible for inspection.
steps.failure_condition: The affected valve cannot be confidently identified from the available source information.

actions.label: Locate affected valve
actions.exact_action: Identify the valve associated with the clogged air line.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use only as a general component-identification aid near the tipper assembly; the packet does not provide a valve-specific cleaning image.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the affected valve cannot be identified from the source-supported information.
Stop or escalate if safe access or maintenance controls are not established, because the source does not provide isolation details.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 6.1: PNEUMATIC TROUBLESHOOTING... Air line clogged... Debris in the small ports inside the valve. Use compressed air to clean out the ports. If contamination is common, a better filtration system may be needed.

steps.title: Inspect the small ports inside the valve for debris
steps.purpose: Confirm the source-listed cause of the clogged air line before cleaning.
steps.instruction: Inspect the small ports inside the identified valve for debris.
steps.expected_result: The presence or absence of debris in the small valve ports is determined.
steps.healthy_condition: The small ports are clear of visible debris.
steps.failure_condition: Debris is present in the small ports, or the ports cannot be adequately inspected with the information provided.

actions.label: Inspect valve ports
actions.exact_action: Inspect the small ports inside the valve for debris.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the valve ports cannot be safely accessed or inspected using source-supported information.
Escalate if contamination appears common or recurring.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Air line clogged... Debris in the small ports inside the valve.

steps.title: Use compressed air to clean out the ports
steps.purpose: Remove debris from the small valve ports to address the clogged air line condition.
steps.instruction: Use compressed air to clean out the small ports inside the valve.
steps.expected_result: Debris is blown out of the small valve ports.
steps.healthy_condition: The ports appear clean after compressed-air cleaning.
steps.failure_condition: Debris remains in the ports after cleaning, or contamination appears recurrent.

actions.label: Clean valve ports with compressed air
actions.exact_action: Use compressed air to clean out the ports.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if contamination is common, because a better filtration system may be needed.
Stop or escalate if the ports cannot be cleaned with the source-supported action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Use compressed air to clean out the ports. If contamination is common, a better filtration system may be needed.

steps.title: Verify whether the clog condition is cleared
steps.purpose: Confirm whether cleaning the valve ports addressed the clogged air line condition.
steps.instruction: Verify whether the clogged air line condition is cleared after cleaning the small valve ports.
steps.expected_result: The clogged air line condition is either cleared or confirmed to persist.
steps.healthy_condition: The clogged air line condition is addressed after the ports are cleaned.
steps.failure_condition: The clogged air line condition remains after cleaning the ports.

actions.label: Verify clog cleared
actions.exact_action: Verify whether the clog condition is cleared.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the clog condition remains after cleaning the ports.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Air line clogged... Debris in the small ports inside the valve. Use compressed air to clean out the ports.

success_criteria: Debris is cleared from the small ports inside the valve.
The clogged air line condition is addressed after cleaning.
healthy_conditions: The small valve ports are clear of debris after cleaning.
The clogged air line condition is no longer present.
failure_conditions: The affected valve cannot be confidently identified.
The small ports cannot be safely accessed or inspected using the source-provided information.
The clogged air line condition remains after cleaning the ports.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if contamination is common, because the source states that a better filtration system may be needed.
Escalate if the clog condition remains after cleaning the ports.
Escalate for SME or maintenance review because the source does not provide isolation, disassembly, or pneumatic safety details.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: General component layout only; no valve-port cleaning image is provided in the packet.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 6.1: PNEUMATIC TROUBLESHOOTING... Air line clogged... Debris in the small ports inside the valve. Use compressed air to clean out the ports. If contamination is common, a better filtration system may be needed.

missing_details: The source does not identify the exact valve location for the clogged air line.
The source does not provide pneumatic isolation or pressure-release instructions.
The source does not provide disassembly or access steps for reaching the small valve ports.
The source does not define a detailed verification method for confirming the clog is cleared.
The packet does not include a valve-specific image for this procedure.
